action.reasoning: Observation: Latest eval shows improved ptend_t (0.59), radiation (0.95), precipitation (0.76) but ptend_q r2 remains extremely negative (~-9.5e9), driving mean_r2 to ~-4e9 (worse than iter3's -2.45e7). Training completes with logged val_mse but q-component fails to fit (likely exploding outputs or loss imbalance). Predict now works with batching. Analysis: Uniform MSE loss lets easy components dominate; q-tendency (60 dims) needs emphasis. Large width/depth + high LR without clipping causes instability. Curves unhelpful due to metric mismatch. Adding q-weighted loss (x5), grad clipping, small output init, lower LR/dropout=0, wider/shallower net (2048/3), val R2 logging (matching eval groups: t[0:60], q[60:120], rad[120,121,124-127], precip[122:124]) will diagnose and target the bottleneck. 30 epochs feasible (~20min). Decision: Update model/train.py accordingly, train, submit_eval on best.pt for new score/curves.
